executive_summary: Canada is experiencing an unprecedented ransomware surge with 100+ organizations compromised in a single 48-hour period across all sectors. High-profile victims include Canada Goose (hit by two separate gangs), Markham Stouffville Hospital, National Money Mart (database leaked), Peterborough Public Health, and the Municipality of North Perth. Qilin ransomware leads the attack wave with 20 confirmed victims, followed by Play, SafePay, and a major Clop campaign targeting 15 organizations. Critical infrastructure sectors including healthcare, education, financial services, and public sector entities are under active assault.

items.targets: Service Star Freightways, Kuzco Lighting, Waterloo Information Systems, A Lococo Wholesale, Traffic Tech, Gradient Wind Engineering, Workers Health & Safety Centre, Bergmanis Preyra, Pre-Con Builders, Questica, Eastern Townships School Board, MG Chartered Professional Accountant, Watermark Beach Resort, CST Coal, BioPharma Services, NovAtel (Hexagon subsidiary), Maheu&Maheu, Berts Electric

canada_landscape.summary: Canada is under severe ransomware attack with over 100 organizations compromised in 48 hours across all provinces and sectors. Critical infrastructure including hospitals (Markham Stouffville), public health (Peterborough), municipal government (North Perth), and education (Eastern Townships School Board, Collge Superieur De Montreal) have been breached. No CCCS advisories were identified in this feed.
canada_landscape.retailers: Canada Goose was hit by two separate ransomware gangs (CoinbaseCartel and ShinyHunters) simultaneously. Kids & Company (childcare services) and Active Green + Ross (automotive retail) were also compromised.
canada_landscape.financial: National Money Mart Company suffered a database leak via Everest ransomware. Morison Insurance Brokers and multiple accounting firms (Pewarchuk CPA, MG Chartered Professional Accountant, Bergmanis Preyra law firm) were compromised.

report_2w.executive_summary: A critical vulnerability (CVE-2026-40175, CVSS 10.0) in the widely-used Axios HTTP library has been discovered, affecting multiple AWS Lambda base images and the OpenAI macOS app signing pipeline—this represents a supply chain compromise with broad potential impact. Canadian Cyber Centre has issued AL26-007 warning of CVE-2026-35616 affecting Fortinet FortiClientEMS. Ransomware group Qilin continues active campaigns with multiple unverified claims, while ShinyHunters is threatening to leak Rockstar Games data via Snowflake/Anodot breach on April 14 if ransom demands aren't met. Iranian-linked MuddyWater APT is conducting espionage operations in the Middle East using Rust and Telegram since January 2026.

items.description: Axios HTTP client library vulnerability enabling remote code execution or complete system compromise
items.software_affected: Axios (prior to 1.15.0), AWS Lambda base images, OpenAI macOS app
items.cia_impact: Confidentiality: High, Integrity: High, Availability: High

items.description: OpenClaw arbitrary code execution vulnerability in local plugin and hook installation
items.cia_impact: Confidentiality: High, Integrity: High, Availability: High

items.description: Chamilo LMS weak password reset mechanism allowing account hijacking using only victim email
items.cia_impact: Confidentiality: High, Integrity: High, Availability: Low
items.access_required: Network / Knowledge of victim email address

items.name: ShinyHunters
items.targets: Rockstar Games (via Anodot/Snowflake breach), Wynn Resorts (21,000 employees affected), Abrigo Inc.
items.ttps: T1589 - Gather Victim Identity Information, T1567 - Exfiltration Over Web Service, T1486 - Data Encrypted for Impact (ransomware/extortion)

items.name: Qilin (ransomware group)
items.targets: Chalmers & Kubeck, Hofland, SAAM Towage (all unverified claims)
items.ttps: T1486 - Data Encrypted for Impact, T1491 - Defacement (leak site posting)

items.name: MuddyWater (Iranian APT, Operation Olalampo)
items.ttps: T1102 - Web Service (Telegram C2), T1059 - Command and Scripting Interpreter (Rust malware), T1071 - Application Layer Protocol

items.name: Handala
items.ttps: T1530 - Data from Cloud Storage, T1567 - Exfiltration Over Web Service

items.name: VENOM (phishing campaign)
items.targets: Senior executives at multiple organizations (5,000+ affected), Dutch hospitals (ChipSoft ransomware affecting 70% of NL hospitals)
items.ttps: T1566 - Phishing, T1078 - Valid Accounts (Microsoft login theft), T1114 - Email Collection

items.name: ShadowLink / TeamPCP
items.ttps: T1584 - Compromise Infrastructure (residential proxy networks), T1195 - Supply Chain Compromise (CI/CD attacks), T1090 - Proxy

items.name: North Korean threat actors (APT)
items.targets: Axios npm supply chain, OpenAI macOS app signing pipeline
items.ttps: T1195.002 - Compromise Software Supply Chain, T1554 - Compromise Client Software Binary (manipulated Axios library)

canada_landscape.summary: The Canadian Centre for Cyber Security (CCCS) issued Alert AL26-007 on April 7, 2026, warning of CVE-2026-35616 affecting Fortinet FortiClientEMS, requiring immediate attention from Canadian organizations using this enterprise endpoint management solution. No specific incidents targeting major Canadian retailers or financial institutions were identified in this reporting period, though general cybersecurity discussions around Canadian policy, data sovereignty, and infrastructure continue.
